# CATL takes strategic stake in CarbonScape biographite firm

CATL's bet on bio-derived graphite targets the battery supply chain's most voluminous yet least scrutinised critical material

* By  [Automotive World](https://www.automotiveworld.com/author/anmol-mothy/)

CATL, the world’s largest battery manufacturer, has taken a strategic stake in CarbonScape Ltd, a New Zealand-founded firm developing bio-based graphite from forestry by-products for lithium-ion battery anodes. The investment gives CATL board representation and establishes it as CarbonScape’s primary industrialisation partner ahead of commercial-scale deployment.

Graphite is the largest single material in a lithium-ion battery cell by volume, representing up to 50% of the cell, yet more than 75% of global battery-grade supply is currently derived from oil-based feedstocks. CarbonScape’s proprietary process converts forestry residues into battery-grade graphite at target cost parity with conventional material. Global demand for battery-grade graphite is forecast to grow approximately six-fold between 2025 and 2040.

Lochpine Capital has co-invested alongside CATL, while existing shareholder Stora Enso retains its stake in CarbonScape. CATL and CarbonScape will validate the process at demonstration scale at CATL facilities before advancing to full commercial plant construction.

In a statement, Ivan Williams, Chief Executive Officer of CarbonScape, said: ‘This partnership is about far more than capital. It provides access to CATL’s unparalleled expertise in scaling and mass production, world-class facilities, global market reach, and a clear pathway to gigafactory-scale deployment. It validates the strategic importance of biographite in the future of electrification and uniquely aligns technical de-risking with route-to-market readiness. Together, we aim to bring commercial biographite production online by the end of the decade.’

Source: [CarbonScape](https://www.carbonscape.com/latest-news/worlds-largest-battery-maker-catl-partners-with-carbonscape)
